items.q: What force keeps a car moving in a circle around a bend?
items.correct: Friction from the road acting towards the centre of the circle — centripetal force.
items.misconception: A centrifugal force pushes the car outwards from the centre.
items.random_wrong: The car's own momentum acts as a force that drives it in a circle.
items.why: Students experience the 'outward push' in a car (a result of inertia in a rotating frame) and interpret it as a real outward force.

items.q: Why do more massive stars have shorter main-sequence lifetimes than smaller ones?
items.correct: They burn through their hydrogen fuel much faster because higher core temperature drives nuclear fusion at a vastly greater rate.
items.misconception: All stars live the same time on the main sequence regardless of mass.
items.random_wrong: More massive stars live longer because they have more fuel.
items.why: Students assume more fuel always means longer life, missing that mass increases consumption rate even more sharply than fuel supply.

items.q: What produces a magnetic field around a wire?
items.correct: A moving electric charge — i.e. an electric current.
items.misconception: The metal in the wire is naturally magnetic.
items.random_wrong: The voltage across the wire creates magnetism.
items.why: Students think magnetism is intrinsic to the wire material rather than a consequence of charge motion.

items.correct: The driving frequency matches the natural frequency.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The driving frequency matches the natural frequency.. “Damping is critical.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Heavy damping in a car suspension causes:
items.correct: Slow return to equilibrium without oscillation.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Slow return to equilibrium without oscillation.. “Continuous oscillation at high amplitude.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
